title: salvia blancoana subsp. maurorum
intro: Salvia blancoana subsp. maurorum is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae), native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, coughs, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Salvia blancoana subsp. maurorum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Salvia blancoana subsp. maurorum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
